text.title: Short-Lived Branch Flow, Branch by Abstraction, Dark Launch
text.summary: Short-lived branch git workflow, Branch by Abstraction for large refactors, Keystone Interface pattern, and dark-launch for production traffic comparison.

example.code:
# Branch from main, work, rebase often, merge same day git checkout -b feat/user-validation git push -u origin feat/user-validation
# Keep branch current git fetch origin main && git rebase origin/main
# Open PR and merge same day gh pr create --title "Add user validation" gh pr merge --squash
# Delete branch immediately git checkout main && git pull git branch -d feat/user-validation

rule.statement: For large refactors, create an abstraction layer first, implement the new version behind it, switch via a flag, validate, then remove the old code — all on trunk.
rule.rationale: Long-lived refactor branches accumulate conflicts and hide intermediate states from CI. Branch by Abstraction keeps all steps reviewable and reversible on trunk.

rule.statement: Dark-launch a new implementation by running it in parallel with the current one; compare results and log differences before switching.
rule.rationale: Production traffic reveals edge cases that unit tests miss. Dark-launching lets you validate correctness without exposing users to risk.
